mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 235 in CPIAUCSL triggers central bank rate hikes, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 235.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and falling interest rates (FEDFUNDS) signals strong economic demand and accommodative policy, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days through improved corporate earnings prospects.
failure_condition: The joint effect of GDPC1 growth and FEDFUNDS decline fails to produce positive returns in > 40% of cases over 60-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: A tightening labor market (UNRATE < 6.5%) reduces wage pressures and corporate costs, boosting profit margins and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 50% of periods when UNRATE remains below 6.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates stronger supply-side capacity utilization, improving corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to correlate with positive SPX returns over 5-day horizons in > 30% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signal a robust labor market, boosting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The combined effect of PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline fails to produce positive returns in > 40% of cases over 20 days.

mechanism_narrative: Growing housing starts (HOUST) reflect increased demand for residential construction, stimulating economic activity and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 50% of periods when HOUST exceeds its 3-month moving average.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL indicate monetary tightening to combat inflation, raising borrowing costs and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of rising FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL fails to produce negative returns in > 40% of cases over 60 days.
